name: Sinequa Enterprise Search and Analytics API
what_it_does: Enterprise search and analytics platform with REST API — enabling large enterprises to search across heterogeneous data sources (SharePoint, Confluence, file systems, databases, emails) with AI-powered natural language understanding, knowledge graph, and semantic search, with a customizable search UI framework and API for building enterprise knowledge portals and cognitive search applications.
use_cases: Building enterprise knowledge portal via Sinequa API for unified search across corporate data sources
Implementing cognitive search via Sinequa for NLP-powered document discovery in large enterprises
Connecting SharePoint, Confluence, and file systems via Sinequa connectors for unified enterprise search
Building pharmaceutical regulatory search via Sinequa for life sciences compliance document discovery
Creating workplace AI assistant via Sinequa API for employee knowledge base querying
not_for: Small to mid-market companies without enterprise data volumes and budgets
E-commerce product search (Sinequa is enterprise unstructured data, not product catalog)
Simple website search (use Algolia or Elasticsearch for smaller-scale search)
best_when: A large enterprise (1,000+ employees) needs cognitive search across hundreds of internal data sources — where Sinequa's NLP, knowledge graph, and connectors provide unified discovery across SharePoint, email, documents, and databases.
avoid_when: You're SMB, need e-commerce product search, or have simple single-source search needs.

auth.notes: OAuth2 PKCE for end-user authentication. JWT token for API calls. Application-level JWT for server-side API access. Integration with enterprise identity: SAML, Active Directory/LDAP, Azure AD. Token-based auth (Bearer JWT). No fine-grained API key scopes — permissions via Sinequa ACLs. Sinequa SBA (Search Based Application) framework with TypeScript SDK. No webhooks.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier; enterprise procurement required
pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise custom pricing; typically $100K-1M+/year based on data volume and users
pricing.notes: French company (Paris). Founded 2002 by Laurent Fanichet. Private — no public funding disclosed. 200+ enterprise customers. Sectors: pharmaceutical, aerospace, financial services, government. Customers: Airbus, Sanofi, AXA, EDF, OECD, NATO. Life sciences specialization (pharmaceutical regulatory compliance). Cognitive Search with NLP (Named Entity Recognition, relationship extraction). On-premises and private cloud deployment. Angular-based SBA framework (TypeScript/JavaScript). 140+ built-in connectors (SharePoint, File System, Web, Email, Databases, SAP, Salesforce). Known for very large-scale text analytics (billions of documents). Gartner Insight Engine leader. Strong European enterprise market.

performance.rate_limits: Enterprise agreement defines limits; not publicly documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API with JWT auth. SAML/AD/Azure AD identity integration. TypeScript SBA SDK. No webhooks. GDPR/ISO27001. French company. Enterprise-only — large annual contract. 140+ connectors. On-premises option for classified data. Limited public API documentation.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Search queries fully idempotent. Document reads idempotent by document ID. Index status reads idempotent. Analytics reads idempotent for same time ranges.
known_agent_gotchas: Enterprise procurement required — no self-service; sales-led engagement and long procurement cycle
JWT token required — obtain via OAuth2 PKCE (user auth) or application credentials (server-side)
SBA framework (TypeScript/Angular) — not REST-only; strongly coupled to Angular front-end framework
On-premises deployment common — API endpoint is your internal Sinequa server URL
Document security trimming: search results filtered by user's AD permissions — test with appropriate user
French company — GDPR by design; DPA required for EU data processing

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR, ISO27001. OAuth2 PKCE + enterprise identity (SAML, AD, Azure AD). ACL-based document security (search results trimmed by user permissions). French company — GDPR by default. On-premises deployment for classified/regulated data. NATO/government customers imply high security standards. Enterprise contract includes DPA. No webhooks (reduces attack surface).
